title: identity — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. #[AsCredentialListener] marks a class as a subscriber to credential-lifecycle events (registered, locked, password changed, MFA enrolled, erased). Discovered at boot by the IdentityServiceProvider so services under `auth/`, `notifications/`, `activity/`, and `observability/` can react without explicit event bindings.

attributes.name: AsCredentialListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which credential events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Academorix\Identity\Events\*`. Specific list e.g. `['IdentityLocked', 'PasswordChanged']` subscribes to those only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. Set to `null` for synchronous listeners (rare — reserve for observability tap-outs). Common values: `notifications` (default, mail/push channels), `notifications-critical` (P0 events like IdentityLocked reaching multiple failed attempts), `audit` (write-heavy compliance streams).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower values run first. Use for cases where one listener's side-effect must precede another (e.g. `PurgeSessionsListener` before `SendPasswordChangedConfirmation`). Framework default listeners occupy 1–99; user-defined listeners default to 100.
